body:
Implemented detached / multiple trigger support in Dialog and AlertDialog, similarly to https://github.com/mui/base-ui/pull/2336

It is now possible to define triggers outside of Dialog.Root (or AlertDialog.Root) using the `handle` prop. Multiple triggers per dialog with a dynamic payload are also implemented.

Couple of additions not present in the Popover:

1. Reactive payload - any changes to the active trigger's `payload` will be reflected in the Dialog.Root's child render function's parameter. This has been also implemented in Popover in this PR.
2. Imperative methods on handles - handles are now separate classes (not just Store instances as before) and expose public `open`, `openWithPayload`, and `close` methods. They can be used to interact with the dialog imperatively, even when in uncontrolled mode. They offer the ability to open a dialog and associate it with a given trigger (setting its ARIA attributes properly), opening a standalone dialog, and optionally providing a custom payload. This should make the controlled mode almost unnecessary and will make it much more convenient to implement scenarios like those described in #2802 and #2069. This will be ported to Popover in a separate PR.

Docs:
https://deploy-preview-2974--base-ui.netlify.app/react/components/dialog#detached-triggers
https://deploy-preview-2974--base-ui.netlify.app/react/components/alert-dialog#detached-triggers
https://deploy-preview-2974--base-ui.netlify.app/experiments/dialog/dialogs

Closes #2802
Closes #2069
title: [dialog][alert dialog] Support detached triggers
